title: Fire Control Electronics Assembly and Test
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 1 — Defence electronics manufacturer attracted through PSAP using Riyadh electronics manufacturing platform
executive_summary:
Fire control systems for armoured vehicles — including the Cockerill 3105 turret's ballistic computer, gunner's sight electronics, commander's panoramic sight, and stabilisation electronics — are high-value, low-volume assemblies that sit at the intersection of precision electronics manufacturing and defence system integration. The electronics are predominantly European-origin (non-ITAR) for Saudi Arabia's wheeled vehicle fleet, making them accessible for in-Kingdom assembly and test without US export control restrictions.

Node LS-4 targets Phase II establishment of a Saudi electronics assembly and test facility capable of producing fire control electronic assemblies to IPC-A-610 Class 3 aerospace/defence standard — the highest commercial electronics quality standard. The facility serves the Cockerill turret programme initially and expands to other vehicle electronics (communications, navigation, driver vision) as Saudi content requirements grow.

The global military electronics assembly market is valued at $12 billion annually. Saudi Arabia's Phase II fire control electronics opportunity is estimated at $40–80 million over the programme lifecycle — small in global terms, but the capability foundation for a Saudi defence electronics manufacturing cluster that can serve all land, naval, and aerospace platforms.

companies.name: Thales
companies.rationale: Thales supplies fire control systems to European armoured vehicle programmes and has Saudi Arabia defence relationships. Thales's non-ITAR fire control electronics are directly relevant to the Cockerill programme.
companies.contact: Thales Land and Air Systems VP Saudi Arabia

companies.name: Elbit Systems (subject to normalisation)
companies.rationale: Elbit is a global leader in turret fire control electronics. Subject to Saudi-Israeli normalisation progress.

companies.name: Hensoldt
companies.rationale: Hensoldt produces gunner's sights and commander's panoramic sights for European vehicle programmes. Non-ITAR German technology with active Gulf defence market engagement.
companies.contact: Hensoldt VP International Programmes, Middle East

companies.name: Lacroix Defence
companies.rationale: French defence electronics specialist with IPC-A-610 Class 3 manufacturing and a track record of international manufacturing partnerships.
companies.contact: Lacroix Defence VP International Industrial Cooperation

companies.name: Curtiss-Wright Defence
companies.rationale: Curtiss-Wright produces rugged military electronics assemblies and has prior Gulf engagement. Some ITAR restrictions apply but non-ITAR product lines exist.

partners.name: SAMI Electronics Division
partners.current: Defence electronics assembly, communications equipment; some board-level assembly
partners.upgrade: IPC-A-610 Class 3 certification; Thales/Hensoldt licensed assembly procedures; dedicated fire control electronics production line; automated optical inspection (AOI) and functional test equipment
partners.timeline: 24 months to first qualified fire control assembly

partners.name: Saudi Telecom (STC) / STC Solutions
partners.current: Large-scale electronics integration; network equipment assembly; some defence communications
partners.upgrade: Establish defence electronics manufacturing subsidiary; IPC-A-610 Class 3; ESD-protected manufacturing environment; NSCS clearance for classified system assembly

partners.name: KACST Electronics Research Institute
partners.current: Electronics research; some defence system integration; signal processing
partners.upgrade: Fire control electronics test laboratory; develop Saudi test procedures for non-ITAR fire control assemblies; support Saudisation of electronics test engineering

partners.name: Elm Company
partners.current: IT systems integration; some hardware assembly for government systems
partners.upgrade: Defence electronics assembly qualification; IPC-A-610 training programme; ESD-protected assembly environment; fire control test equipment procurement
